---
output:
  beamer_presentation:
    theme: "CambridgeUS"
    colortheme: "dolphin"
    fonttheme: "structurebold"
fontsize: 14pt
classoption: "aspectratio=169"
header-includes:
- \usepackage{caption}
- \captionsetup[figure]{labelformat=empty}
- \captionsetup[table]{labelformat=empty}
- \setbeamertemplate{page number in head/foot}[]{}
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
## Render the pdf
##rmarkdown::render(input = "./04_3-Exploring_PD_Strategies.Rmd", output_file = "./04_3-Exploring_PD_Strategies.pdf")

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```


## Today's Agenda
\Large

Thinking Like a Political Scientist: Interests, Institutions and Interactions

\vspace{.5in}

\begin{center}
Justin Leinaweaver (Fall 2021)
\end{center}


## {.plain}
\textbf{A Prisoner's Dilemma}

+ Each round your group must choose to cooperate or defect.
+ Your reward depends on the choice made by the other group.

\begin{center}
\includegraphics[keepaspectratio=true,width=.92\paperwidth]{Images/04_1-PD_Table.png}
\end{center}


## Prisoner's Dilemma Strategies
\LARGE

+ Unconditional Cooperator (UC)
+ Unconditional Defector (UD)
+ Probability p Cooperator (PC)
+ Tit for Tat (TT)
+ Grim Trigger (GT)




## For Today
\Large

Bring to class a current international political event that illustrates the logic of the PD game or the role of interests, institutions and interactions in action.



## {.plain}
\begin{center}
\includegraphics[keepaspectratio=true,width=.92\paperwidth]{Images/04_3-intro_fuhrmann_kreps.png}
\end{center}
